assertions.expectation: Configures a recurring maintenance window for weekends only
assertions.reason: Response provides recurring weekend maintenance windows using Saturday schedule with --maintenance-window-recurrence "FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SA"

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command with --maintenance-window-start/end and --maintenance-window-recurrence for weekly weekend schedule
assertions.reason: Multiple gcloud commands show --maintenance-window-start, --maintenance-window-end, and --maintenance-window-recurrence with proper weekly Saturday schedule format

assertions.expectation: Sets up maintenance exclusions for quarterly code freezes (up to 30 days each)
assertions.reason: Provides specific example for Q4 code freeze with --add-maintenance-exclusion commands and mentions quarterly code freeze handling

assertions.expectation: Sets up a maintenance exclusion for the November audit period
assertions.reason: Includes specific section with gcloud command for November audit exclusion: "annual-audit-nov" from Nov 1-30

assertions.expectation: Notes the 30-day maximum for maintenance exclusions and how to handle longer freezes
assertions.reason: Explicitly mentions "30-day maximum per exclusion" and suggests "Chain exclusions if audit period exceeds 30 days"

assertions.expectation: Explains that manual upgrades bypass maintenance windows — only auto-upgrades respect them
assertions.reason: In manual upgrade section, notes "Trigger manual upgrade (bypasses all maintenance controls)" clearly explaining the bypass behavior

assertions.expectation: Recommends combining maintenance windows with Stable or Extended channel for maximum upgrade control
assertions.reason: Recommends Extended channel throughout and explains the rationale: "Extended channel + no minor or node upgrades exclusion approach" for maximum control

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for each exclusion (--add-maintenance-exclusion-name/start-time/end-time)
assertions.reason: Multiple gcloud commands show --add-maintenance-exclusion-name, --add-maintenance-exclusion-start-time, and --add-maintenance-exclusion-end-time parameters

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risk of falling too far behind on versions during long exclusion periods
assertions.reason: While the response mentions Extended channel support and end-of-support tracking, it does not explicitly warn about risks of falling behind on versions during long exclusion periods
